leadText: Tinkatink (Japanese: カヌチャン Kanuchan) is a dual-type Fairy/Steel Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It evolves into Tinkatuff starting at level 24, which evolves into Tinkaton starting at level 38.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Tinkatink forging a new hammer in order to evolve Tinkatink is a short, pink, humanoid Pokémon, with a relatively large head and arms compared to the rest of its body and stumpy toeless feet. Its arms have three fingers each, and it has what appears to be a pyramidal diamond-shaped steel plate on its torso. Its head has light pink circles on its cheeks, silver eyes, and a mouth with a square teeth-like growth on the lower lip. It has thin eyebrows or eyelashes above its eyes that give it a perpetually worried expression. On top of its head is a light pink cap with a white protrusion on top, resembling tied hair. Tinkatink carries a crude, gray, cylindrical hammer with a short handle, which resembles a baby's rattle. It creates its own hammer using iron scraps, remaking it as many times as it wants until it is satisfied. Tinkatink uses tools and metal fragments it finds in mines, steel factories or anywhere to help forge this special hammer. Once it's new hammer is made Tinkatink likely discards it's old one to be eaten or used by other Tinkatink. Thanks to it's tendency for metal, it can tell when a piece of scrap metal is either good or not. Additionally, Tinkatink usually gathers in groups to learn the skills needed for pounding and shaping metal from a Tinkaton . Tinkatink uses the hammer to protect itself. However, the hammer is often stolen and eaten by Pokémon with a metal diet seen in Tinkatink's Ideal Hammer . As depicted in Tinkatink's Hammer Wasn't Made in a Day , forging the hammer will trigger Tinkatink's Evolution into Tinkatuff . Tinkatink is a female-only species. Tinkatink resides in various ruins across Paldea . Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Tinkatink evolves into Tinkatuff , which evolves into Tinkaton . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Tinkatink Fairy Steel → First Evolution Tinkatuff Fairy Steel → Second Evolution Tinkaton Fairy Steel

Generation IX Paldea #279 Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #016 Scarlet It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal. Violet This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it's satisfied with the result. Legends: Z-A Tinkatink cobbles together a suitable hammer for itself using tools and metal fragments it finds in mines. Spin-off games Pokopia #268 Pokopia It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry HZ039 Tinkatink Dot's Rotom Phone Tinkatink, the Metalsmith Pokémon. A Fairy and Steel type. It pounds scrap metal into a hammer. It will make new hammers over and over again until it is satisfied.
